output:
7-Day Gluten-Free, Dairy-Free, Onion-Free Meal Plan  
(Uses mild, adjustable seasoning; avoid very salty/sweet; budget-friendly)

Day 1  
 Breakfast  
  • Overnight oats: gluten-free oats, almond milk, cinnamon, sliced banana  
 Lunch  
  • Chicken salad: shredded roast chicken, mixed greens, cucumber, tomato, olive-oil & lemon dressing  
 Dinner  
  • Thai-style coconut chicken soup: chicken pieces, sliced carrot, capsicum, garlic, ginger, light coconut milk, rice noodles (season with fish sauce*, lime, mild chilli to taste)  
 Snack  
  • Apple slices + handful almonds  
 Prep note  
  • Soak oats overnight. Roast/braise extra chicken for salads and Day 3. Cook extra rice noodles.

Day 2  
 Breakfast  
  • Green smoothie: spinach, frozen berries, banana, almond milk, a pinch of cinnamon  
 Lunch  
  • Beef & black-bean rice bowl: ground beef (mildly seasoned with cumin, paprika), canned black beans (rinsed), chopped tomato, avocado, squeeze of lime  
 Dinner  
  • Indian-style chicken & potato curry: chicken pieces, cubed potato, peas, garlic, ginger, turmeric, mild curry powder, light coconut milk; serve with steamed rice  
 Snack  
  • Carrot sticks + homemade hummus (chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon, olive oil, salt)  
 Prep note  
  • Cook extra rice for Day 4. Make hummus in advance.

Day 3  
 Breakfast  
  • Scrambled eggs with baby spinach and cherry-tomato halves  
 Lunch  
  • GF chicken tacos: warmed corn tortillas, shredded leftover roast chicken, lettuce, tomato, avocado, lime; optional mild salsa  
 Dinner  
  • Potato & capsicum hash: diced potato & capsicum pan-fried in oil, seasoned with paprika, garlic, salt & pepper; serve with a fried egg (or two)  
 Snack  
  • GF rice cakes + peanut butter  
 Prep note  
  • Dice potatoes/capsicum and store in fridge.

Day 4  
 Breakfast  
  • Chia pudding: chia seeds soaked overnight in coconut milk, topped with fresh berries or banana slices  
 Lunch  
  • Warm potato & egg salad: boiled potatoes, chopped hard-boiled eggs, chopped parsley/cilantro, olive oil, mustard, lemon  
 Dinner  
  • Mexican lentil chili: red lentils, canned tomatoes, diced capsicum, garlic, cumin, coriander, mild chilli powder; serve with corn tortilla chips  
 Snack  
  • Banana + handful walnuts  
 Prep note  
  • Pre-cook red lentils.

Day 5  
 Breakfast  
  • GF toast with mashed avocado, tomato slices, pinch of salt & pepper  
 Lunch  
  • Thai peanut chicken lettuce wraps: minced chicken stir-fried with grated carrot & garlic, tossed with a mild peanut sauce (peanut butter, light soy-free tamari, lime, water)  
 Dinner  
  • Simple dhal: red lentils cooked with turmeric, garlic, ginger; finish with a splash of coconut milk; serve with rice or leftover GF bread  
 Snack  
  • Cucumber sticks + guacamole (avocado, tomato, lime, garlic)  
 Prep note  
  • Stir-fry extra minced chicken for Day 7.

Day 6  
 Breakfast  
  • Mango-coconut smoothie bowl: frozen mango, coconut milk, topped with chia or sliced banana  
 Lunch  
  • Leftover dhal or chicken curry, reheated, served with rice or lettuce wraps  
 Dinner  
  • Beef & potato stir-fry: sliced beef, diced potato (pre-boiled), capsicum, garlic, light soy-free tamari, mild chilli to taste; serve over rice  
 Snack  
  • Mixed nuts & dried fruit  
 Prep note  
  • Pre-boil potatoes for faster stir-fry.

Day 7  
 Breakfast  
  • Omelette: eggs, diced capsicum & tomato, chopped cilantro  
 Lunch  
  • Chicken & potato patties: leftover shredded chicken + mashed potato + egg + gluten-free flour (or ground oats) → pan-fry; serve with side salad  
 Dinner  
  • Mexican stuffed peppers: halved capsicum filled with rice, black beans, tomato, cumin, coriander; bake until tender  
 Snack  
  • Fresh fruit salad (banana, apple, berries)  
 Prep note  
  • Mash leftover potatoes; core peppers in advance.

Grocery List

Produce  
 • Bananas (7)  
 • Apples (3-4)  
 • Mixed berries (fresh or frozen)  
 • Mango (frozen or fresh)  
 • Spinach (1 bag)  
 • Mixed salad greens or lettuce (1 head)  
 • Cucumber (2)  
 • Tomatoes (6-8)  
 • Cherry tomatoes (1 punnet)  
 • Capsicum/bell peppers (6)  
 • Carrots (1 bag)  
 • Potatoes (1.5 kg)  
 • Avocados (4)  
 • Limes (3)  
 • Garlic (1 bulb)  
 • Fresh ginger (small piece)  
 • Fresh cilantro/coriander (1 bunch)  

Proteins & Dairy Alternatives  
 • Chicken thighs or breasts (1.2 kg)  
 • Minced beef (or turkey) (500 g)  
 • Eggs (12)  
 • Canned black beans (2 cans)  
 • Canned chickpeas (1 can)  
 • Red lentils (500 g)  
 • Almond milk (1 L)  
 • Light coconut milk (2 cans)  
 • Peanut butter (no added sugar/salt)  

Grains & Snacks  
 • Gluten-free oats  
 • Gluten-free bread or wraps (1 loaf/pack)  
 • Corn tortillas (1 pack)  
 • Rice (1 kg)  
 • Rice noodles (200 g)  
 • GF rice cakes  
 • Chia seeds  
 • Mixed nuts (almonds, walnuts, etc.)  
 • Dried fruit or raisins  
 • Corn tortilla chips (optional)  

Oils & Condiments  
 • Olive oil  
 • Canola or vegetable oil  
 • Tahini  
 • Soy-free tamari (gluten-free) or GF soy sauce  
 • Mustard (check for gluten)  

Spices & Seasonings  
 • Salt (use sparingly)  
 • Black pepper  
 • Cinnamon  
 • Cumin powder  
 • Coriander powder  
 • Turmeric  
 • Paprika  
 • Mild chilli powder (optional)  
 • Curry powder (mild)  
 • Garam masala  
 • Dried oregano  

Basic Weekly Prep Notes  
 1. Cook a large batch of rice; store in fridge up to 4 days.  
 2. Pre-soak chia and overnight oats.  
 3. Roast/braise half the chicken; reserve for salads and tacos.  
 4. Pre-boil potatoes for quicker hashes, patties and stir-fries.  
 5. Pre-cook red lentils for dhal and chili.  
 6. Chop all veggies (capsicum, carrots, cucumbers, potatoes) on Day 1; keep in sealed containers.  
 7. Make hummus and guacamole early in the week; stir before serving.

Seasoning tip: always start with small amounts of salt, chilli or spice blends; let each eater adjust to taste.  
Enjoy simple, flavourful meals that fit your dietary needs and budget!

## 7‑Day “Allergy‑Safe, Budget‑Friendly” Meal Plan  
*For a 31‑year‑old Australian adult – shellfish‑free, gluten‑free, lactose‑free, onion‑free*  

### How the plan meets the brief  

| Requirement | How it’s handled |
|-------------|-----------------|
| **Allergies / Intolerances** | No shellfish, wheat‑based products, dairy, or onion appear in any recipe. |
| **Flavor preferences** | Mexican, Thai and Indian spice profiles are used, but all are **mild** (no heavy chili, no extra salt). Seasonings are added at the table so the eater can adjust. |
| **Budget / Simplicity** | Core pantry items – rice, potatoes, canned beans, lentils, frozen veg, basic spices – keep costs low. Most meals can be assembled in ≤ 30 min, and several components are cooked in bulk on the weekend. |
| **Super‑taster sensitivity** | Flavors are kept gentle – a pinch of salt, a drizzle of lime, a sprinkle of fresh herbs – and each dish is served with the seasoning on the side. |
| **Balanced nutrition** | Each day includes protein, complex carbs, veg and a fruit/healthy‑fat snack. |

---

## DAILY MENU  

*(All portions are for one adult. Adjust portion sizes up or down as needed.)*

### Day 1
| Meal | Dish (ingredients) | Quick notes |
|------|--------------------|-------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Gluten‑free oat porridge** – ½ cup rolled oats, 1 cup almond milk, sliced banana, a pinch cinnamon. | Microwave 2 min; stir. |
| **Snack** | Apple slices + 2 tbsp sunflower‑seed butter. | |
| **Lunch** | **Mexican‑style rice bowl** – 1 cup cooked brown rice, ½ cup black beans (rinsed), ¼ cup corn, diced red capsicum, ½ avocado, lime wedge, chopped coriander. | Drizzle olive oil & a pinch salt at table. |
| **Snack** | Handful of unsalted mixed nuts. | |
| **Dinner** | **Mild Thai chicken coconut soup (Tom Kha Gai – onion‑free)** – 150 g chicken breast, 1 cup coconut milk, 1 cup chicken broth (low‑salt), ½ cup sliced mushrooms, ½ cup sliced carrot, 1 tsp grated ginger, 1 tsp lime juice, a few coriander leaves. | Simmer 15 min; serve with a side of steamed rice (½ cup). |

---

### Day 2
| Meal | Dish | Quick notes |
|------|------|------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Scrambled eggs & spinach** – 2 eggs, handful fresh spinach, 1 tsp olive oil, pinch pepper. | Cook on low heat, fold in spinach just before done. |
| **Snack** | 1 medium orange. | |
| **Lunch** | **Indian lentil dal** – ½ cup red lentils, 1 cup water, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp cumin, ¼ tsp mild chili powder, pinch salt, 1 tsp garlic‑infused oil (no onion). Serve with ½ cup cooked basmati rice. | Cook lentils 15 min; keep seasoning light, add extra lime at table if desired. |
| **Snack** | Carrot sticks + 3 tbsp homemade hummus (see prep notes – onion‑free). | |
| **Dinner** | **Beef & potato stir‑fry** – 150 g sliced beef, 1 medium potato (peeled & diced), 1 cup frozen mixed Asian veg (e.g., peas, carrots, baby corn), 1 tsp ginger, 1 tsp low‑salt soy sauce (gluten‑free), 1 tsp sesame oil. | Stir‑fry on high heat 8‑10 min; serve with a small side of rice if hungry. |

---

### Day 3
| Meal | Dish | Quick notes |
|------|------|------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Sweet potato & banana mash** – ½ large baked sweet potato, ½ banana, sprinkle of cinnamon. | Mash together, eat warm. |
| **Snack** | 1 cup grapes. | |
| **Lunch** | **Mexican quinoa salad** – ½ cup cooked quinoa, ¼ cup black beans, ¼ cup corn, diced red capsicum, ¼ avocado, cilantro, lime juice. | Drizzle a tiny bit of olive oil; keep salt on the side. |
| **Snack** | Rice cakes (plain) topped with 2 tbsp almond butter. | |
| **Dinner** | **Chicken tikka‑masala (mild)** – 150 g chicken thigh, ½ cup canned diced tomatoes (no added salt), ½ cup coconut milk, ½ tsp mild garam masala, ¼ tsp turmeric, pinch salt, 1 tsp garlic‑infused oil. Serve with ½ cup cooked basmati rice. | Simmer 20 min; garnish with fresh cilantro. |

---

### Day 4
| Meal | Dish | Quick notes |
|------|------|------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Gluten‑free banana pancakes** – ½ cup gluten‑free oat flour, 1 egg, ¼ cup almond milk, ½ ripe banana mashed, pinch baking powder. Cook 2‑minute pancakes on a non‑stick pan. | Serve with a drizzle of maple syrup (optional, keep light). |
| **Snack** | Handful of pumpkin seeds. | |
| **Lunch** | **Thai‑style beef lettuce wraps** – 150 g minced beef, 1 tsp ginger, 1 tsp fish sauce (gluten‑free), 1 tsp lime juice, shredded carrot, lettuce leaves. | Cook beef, spoon into lettuce, add lime at table. |
| **Snack** | Sliced cucumber + 2 tbsp tahini dip (tahini + lemon juice + water). | |
| **Dinner** | **Baked potato with chili‑style topping** – 1 large potato baked, topped with ½ cup cooked lean ground turkey, ¼ cup canned kidney beans, ¼ cup diced tomatoes, pinch cumin, pinch smoked paprika. | Add a dollop of lactose‑free yoghurt (optional) or a squeeze of lime. |

---

### Day 5
| Meal | Dish | Quick notes |
|------|------|------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Rice porridge (Congee)** – ½ cup cooked white rice, 1 ½ cups water, pinch salt, 1 tsp grated ginger, 1 boiled egg sliced on top. | Simmer 10 min, stir occasionally. |
| **Snack** | 1 pear. | |
| **Lunch** | **Indian spiced chickpea salad** – ½ cup canned chickpeas (rinsed), diced cucumber, diced tomato, ¼ tsp cumin, pinch coriander, lemon juice, chopped mint. | Light dressing, salt on the side. |
| **Snack** | 2 hard‑boiled eggs (season with pepper). | |
| **Dinner** | **Thai green curry (mild) with pork** – 150 g pork tenderloin strips, 1 cup coconut milk, 1 tbsp green curry paste (check for onion‑free), ½ cup sliced zucchini, ½ cup sliced bell pepper, 1 tsp fish sauce, lime wedge. Serve with ½ cup jasmine rice. | Use only ½ tsp paste for mild heat; add extra lime if desired. |

---

### Day 6
| Meal | Dish | Quick notes |
|------|------|------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Savory quinoa bowl** – ½ cup cooked quinoa, 1 egg fried, ¼ cup steamed broccoli, pinch salt & pepper, drizzle of olive oil. | Quick assembly. |
| **Snack** | Handful of dried apricots (unsweetened). | |
| **Lunch** | **Mexican cauliflower rice** – 1 cup cauliflower “rice” (processed), ¼ cup black beans, ¼ cup corn, diced red capsicum, cilantro, lime. | Sauté cauliflower rice 5 min, season lightly. |
| **Snack** | Celery sticks + 2 tbsp almond butter. | |
| **Dinner** | **Shepherd’s pie (potato‑topped)** – 150 g lean ground beef, ½ cup mixed frozen peas & carrots, ¼ cup tomato puree (no added salt), 1 tsp dried oregano, 1 tsp garlic‑infused oil, topped with 1 cup mashed potatoes (made with a splash of lactose‑free milk or water). | Bake 20 min at 180 °C; serve with a side salad (lettuce, cucumber). |

## 7‑Day “Allergy‑Safe, Budget‑Friendly” Meal Plan

### How the plan meets the brief  

| Requirement | How it’s handled |
|-------------|-----------------|
| **Allergies / Intolerances** | No shellfish, wheat‑based products, dairy, or onion appear in any recipe. |
| **Flavor preferences** | Mexican, Thai and Indian spice profiles are used, but all are **mild** (no heavy chili, no extra salt). Seasonings are added at the table so the eater can adjust. |
| **Budget / Simplicity** | Core pantry items – rice, potatoes, canned beans, lentils, frozen veg, basic spices – keep costs low. Most meals can be assembled in ≤ 30 min, and several components are cooked in bulk on the weekend. |
| **Super‑taster sensitivity** | Flavors are kept gentle – a pinch of salt, a drizzle of lime, a sprinkle of fresh herbs – and each dish is served with the seasoning on the side. |
| **Balanced nutrition** | Each day includes protein, complex carbs, veg and a fruit/healthy‑fat snack. |

## DAILY MENU  

*(All portions are for one adult. Adjust portion sizes up or down as needed.)*

### Day 1
| Meal | Dish (ingredients) | Quick notes |
|------|--------------------|-------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Gluten‑free oat porridge** – ½ cup rolled oats, 1 cup almond milk, sliced banana, a pinch cinnamon. | Microwave 2 min; stir. |
| **Snack** | Apple slices + 2 tbsp sunflower‑seed butter. | |
| **Lunch** | **Mexican‑style rice bowl** – 1 cup cooked brown rice, ½ cup black beans (rinsed), ¼ cup corn, diced red capsicum, ½ avocado, lime wedge, chopped coriander. | Drizzle olive oil & a pinch salt at table. |
| **Snack** | Handful of unsalted mixed nuts. | |
| **Dinner** | **Mild Thai chicken coconut soup (Tom Kha Gai – onion‑free)** – 150 g chicken breast, 1 cup coconut milk, 1 cup chicken broth (low‑salt), ½ cup sliced mushrooms, ½ cup sliced carrot, 1 tsp grated ginger, 1 tsp lime juice, a few coriander leaves. | Simmer 15 min; serve with a side of steamed rice (½ cup). |

---

### Day 2
| Meal | Dish | Quick notes |
|------|------|------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Scrambled eggs & spinach** – 2 eggs, handful fresh spinach, 1 tsp olive oil, pinch pepper. | Cook on low heat, fold in spinach just before done. |
| **Snack** | 1 medium orange. | |
| **Lunch** | **Indian lentil dal** – ½ cup red lentils, 1 cup water, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp cumin, ¼ tsp mild chili powder, pinch salt, 1 tsp garlic‑infused oil (no onion). Serve with ½ cup cooked basmati rice. | Cook lentils 15 min; keep seasoning light, add extra lime at table if desired. |
| **Snack** | Carrot sticks + 3 tbsp homemade hummus (see prep notes – onion‑free). | |
| **Dinner** | **Beef & potato stir‑fry** – 150 g sliced beef, 1 medium potato (peeled & diced), 1 cup frozen mixed Asian veg (e.g., peas, carrots, baby corn), 1 tsp ginger, 1 tsp low‑salt soy sauce (gluten‑free), 1 tsp sesame oil. | Stir‑fry on high heat 8‑10 min; serve with a small side of rice if hungry. |

---

### Day 3
| Meal | Dish | Quick notes |
|------|------|------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Sweet potato & banana mash** – ½ large baked sweet potato, ½ banana, sprinkle of cinnamon. | Mash together, eat warm. |
| **Snack** | 1 cup grapes. | |
| **Lunch** | **Mexican quinoa salad** – ½ cup cooked quinoa, ¼ cup black beans, ¼ cup corn, diced red capsicum, ¼ avocado, cilantro, lime juice. | Drizzle a tiny bit of olive oil; keep salt on the side. |
| **Snack** | Rice cakes (plain) topped with 2 tbsp almond butter. | |
| **Dinner** | **Chicken tikka‑masala (mild)** – 150 g chicken thigh, ½ cup canned diced tomatoes (no added salt), ½ cup coconut milk, ½ tsp mild garam masala, ¼ tsp turmeric, pinch salt, 1 tsp garlic‑infused oil. Serve with ½ cup cooked basmati rice. | Simmer 20 min; garnish with fresh cilantro. |

---

### Day 4
| Meal | Dish | Quick notes |
|------|------|------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Gluten‑free banana pancakes** – ½ cup gluten‑free oat flour, 1 egg, ¼ cup almond milk, ½ ripe banana mashed, pinch baking powder. Cook 2‑minute pancakes on a non‑stick pan. | Serve with a drizzle of maple syrup (optional, keep light). |
| **Snack** | Handful of pumpkin seeds. | |
| **Lunch** | **Thai‑style beef lettuce wraps** – 150 g minced beef, 1 tsp ginger, 1 tsp fish sauce (gluten‑free), 1 tsp lime juice, shredded carrot, lettuce leaves. | Cook beef, spoon into lettuce, add lime at table. |
| **Snack** | Sliced cucumber + 2 tbsp tahini dip (tahini + lemon juice + water). | |
| **Dinner** | **Baked potato with chili‑style topping** – 1 large potato baked, topped with ½ cup cooked lean ground turkey, ¼ cup canned kidney beans, ¼ cup diced tomatoes, pinch cumin, pinch smoked paprika. | Add a dollop of lactose‑free yoghurt (optional) or a squeeze of lime. |

---

### Day 5
| Meal | Dish | Quick notes |
|------|------|------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Rice porridge (Congee)** – ½ cup cooked white rice, 1 ½ cups water, pinch salt, 1 tsp grated ginger, 1 boiled egg sliced on top. | Simmer 10 min, stir occasionally. |
| **Snack** | 1 pear. | |
| **Lunch** | **Indian spiced chickpea salad** – ½ cup canned chickpeas (rinsed), diced cucumber, diced tomato, ¼ tsp cumin, pinch coriander, lemon juice, chopped mint. | Light dressing, salt on the side. |
| **Snack** | 2 hard‑boiled eggs (season with pepper). | |
| **Dinner** | **Thai green curry (mild) with pork** – 150 g pork tenderloin strips, 1 cup coconut milk, 1 tbsp green curry paste (check for onion‑free), ½ cup sliced zucchini, ½ cup sliced bell pepper, 1 tsp fish sauce, lime wedge. Serve with ½ cup jasmine rice. | Use only ½ tsp paste for mild heat; add extra lime if desired. |

---

### Day 6
| Meal | Dish | Quick notes |
|------|------|------------|
| **Breakfast** | **Savory quinoa bowl** – ½ cup cooked quinoa, 1 egg fried, ¼ cup steamed broccoli, pinch salt & pepper, drizzle of olive oil. | Quick assembly. |
| **Snack** | Handful of dried apricots (unsweetened). | |
| **Lunch** | **Mexican cauliflower rice** – 1 cup cauliflower “rice” (processed), ¼ cup black beans, ¼ cup corn, diced red capsicum, cilantro, lime. | Sauté cauliflower rice 5 min, season lightly. |
| **Snack** | Celery sticks + 2 tbsp almond butter. | |
| **Dinner** | **Shepherd’s pie (potato‑topped)** – 150 g lean ground beef, ½ cup mixed frozen peas & carrots, ¼ cup tomato puree (no added salt), 1 tsp dried oregano, 1 tsp garlic‑infused oil, topped with 1 cup mashed potatoes (made with a splash of lactose‑free milk or water). | Bake 20 min at 180 °C; serve with a side salad (lettuce, cucumber). |

### **Important Notes Before You Start**

*   **Gluten-Free:** Always check labels on sauces, spices, and processed foods for hidden gluten. Use certified gluten-free tamari or soy sauce.
*   **Lactose-Free:** This plan avoids dairy. For any optional creamy elements, use lactose-free alternatives like coconut cream/milk or lactose-free yogurts.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** These are avoided entirely. Flavor comes from other aromatics like celery, leek (green part only), fennel, and asafoetida (a great garlic/onion substitute used in Indian cooking, found in spice aisles).
*   **Super-Taster & Seasoning:** Seasonings are added in two stages: a mild base during cooking and an adjustable finish at the table. This allows you to control the intensity.
*   **Salt & Sugar:** Recipes rely on herbs, spices, and acids (lemon/lime juice) for flavor instead of salt and sugar.

### **The 7-Day Meal Plan**

**Day 1**
*   **Breakfast:** Scrambled eggs with spinach and a side of sliced tomato.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Mexican beef from last night's dinner, served over a green salad with a lime juice and olive oil dressing.
*   **Dinner:** **Simple Beef Tacos.** Browned minced beef seasoned with cumin, paprika, and a pinch of oregano. Serve with corn tortillas, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, and avocado.
*   **Snack:** A pear and a handful of almonds.

**Day 2**
*   **Breakfast:** Smoothie made with frozen banana, spinach, lactose-free milk (e.g., oat or almond), and a scoop of peanut butter.
*   **Lunch:** **"Buddha Bowl"** with leftover Thai pork, brown rice, shredded carrots, cucumber, and a drizzle of lime juice.
*   **Dinner:** **Mild Thai Basil Pork (Pad Kra Pao).** Fry minced pork with finely chopped celery stalk. Add mild red chili (optional), gluten-free fish sauce, and a pinch of sugar. Stir in fresh basil at the end. Serve with jasmine rice.
*   **Snack:** Rice cakes with avocado.

**Day 3**
*   **Breakfast:** Gluten-free oats made with water or lactose-free milk, topped with sliced banana and a sprinkle of cinnamon.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Thai pork from last night, wrapped in large lettuce leaves.
*   **Dinner:** **Sheet Tray Sausage & Veggies.** Combine gluten-free sausages, chopped potatoes, bell peppers, and zucchini. Toss with olive oil, rosemary, and black pepper. Roast until cooked through.
*   **Snack:** An apple.

**Day 4**
*   **Breakfast:** Two-egg omelette filled with leftover roasted veggies from last night.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover sausage and veggies from the sheet tray dinner.
*   **Dinner:** **Simple Mild Butter Chicken (using coconut cream).** Sauté chicken thigh pieces with ginger, garlic-infused oil, and a blend of mild curry powder, turmeric, and coriander. Stir in a can of tomato puree and a can of coconut cream. Simmer until cooked. Serve with basmati rice.
*   **Snack:** A handful of baby carrots.

**Day 5**
*   **Breakfast:** Smoothie (repeat Day 2).
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Butter Chicken and rice.
*   **Dinner:** **Hearty Beef & Potato Soup.** Brown stewing beef, then add water, diced potatoes, carrots, celery, and a bay leaf. Simmer until beef is tender. Season with black pepper and parsley.
*   **Snack:** A orange.

**Day 6**
*   **Breakfast:** Gluten-free toast with peanut butter and sliced banana.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover beef and potato soup.
*   **Dinner:** **Fajita Night.** Strips of chicken breast or thigh marinated in lime juice, cumin, and paprika. Pan-fry with strips of bell pepper and celery. Serve with corn tortillas, guacamole, and lactose-free sour cream (if available).
*   **Snack:** A handful of walnuts.

**Day 7**
*   **Breakfast:** Scrambled eggs and sliced avocado.
*   **Lunch:** Use leftover fajita fillings to make a salad.
*   **Dinner:** **Roast Chicken & Veggies.** Roast a whole chicken or chicken pieces surrounded by potatoes, carrots, and sweet potato. Season with lemon juice, olive

Here is a cheap, simple, and customizable 7-day meal plan tailored for an Australian adult. It focuses on budget-friendly staples (mince, chicken, potatoes, rice) and uses leftovers for lunches to save time and money. 

Because commercial spice blends and curry pastes almost always contain onion powder or gluten, this plan uses a few basic, mild pantry spices to safely create Mexican, Indian, and Thai flavor profiles.

### **The 7-Day Meal Plan**

**Day 1**
*   **Breakfast:** Certified Gluten-Free (GF) oats (or rice flakes) made with lactose-free (LF) milk, topped with sliced banana and a pinch of cinnamon.
*   **Lunch:** Quick Beef & Potato Hash (pan-fry diced potatoes and a little beef mince).
*   **Dinner:** **Mild Mexican Beef Bowls.** Beef mince cooked with diced tomatoes, mild cumin, and paprika. Served over rice with a sprinkle of LF cheese. *(Cook extra for leftovers).*
*   **Snacks:** Apple slices with peanut butter.

**Day 2**
*   **Breakfast:** 2 scrambled eggs and spinach on GF toast.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Mild Mexican Beef Bowl.
*   **Dinner:** **Mild Indian Chicken & Potatoes.** Diced chicken breast and potatoes simmered in coconut milk, turmeric, ground coriander, and fresh ginger. Served with rice. *(Cook extra for leftovers).*
*   **Snacks:** Rice crackers with a slice of LF cheese.

**Day 3**
*   **Breakfast:** Certified GF oats with LF milk and banana.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Indian Chicken & Potatoes.
*   **Dinner:** **Mexican Potato Tacos.** GF corn tortillas filled with leftover Mexican beef mince, roasted diced potatoes, and fresh tomato.
*   **Snacks:** Carrot sticks with peanut butter.

**Day 4**
*   **Breakfast:** 2 fried eggs with pan-fried tomato and GF toast.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Mexican Potato Tacos (deconstructed into a bowl).
*   **Dinner:** **Thai-style Coconut Chicken.** Chicken pan-fried with zucchini, capsicum, mild ginger, a splash of low-sodium Tamari

Below is a 7‑day meal plan designed for a 31‐year‑old Australian adult who must avoid shellfish, gluten‐containing foods, lactose‐containing dairy, and onion. This plan favors meat and potatoes and draws inspiration from Mexican, Thai and Indian cuisines using mild, adjustable seasonings. All recipes use simple ingredients, and any spice blend or heat level can be easily adjusted at the table. Each day includes breakfast, lunch, dinner and one simple snack. At the end you’ll find a consolidated grocery list and brief prep notes.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 1
──────────────────────────────
Breakfast: Banana Oat Porridge  
• Made with certified gluten‑free oats and warmed with lactose‑free milk. Top with a sprinkle of cinnamon and a few chopped nuts (almonds or walnuts).

Lunch: Grilled Chicken & Roasted Potato Salad  
• Marinate chicken breast in a drizzle of olive oil, lemon juice and a pinch of cumin (mild). Grill and serve with roasted potato cubes, steamed green beans, and shredded carrot. Dress with extra lemon and olive oil.

Dinner: Mild Indian Chicken Curry with Rice  
• Use diced chicken breast, diced potatoes and tomatoes with spices like turmeric, cumin and coriander (add ginger and garlic for extra flavour, if tolerated). Simmer in a light lactose‑free coconut milk base and serve with steamed basmati rice.

Snack: Apple Slices with Almond Butter  
• Simple, naturally sweet apple paired with a tablespoon of almond butter.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 2
──────────────────────────────
Breakfast: Gluten‑Free Toast with Avocado & Tomato  
• Toast gluten‑free bread, spread mashed avocado, and top with thin tomato slices and a sprinkle of salt and pepper (on the side so seasoning can be adjusted).

Lunch: Thai‑Inspired Chicken Lettuce Wraps  
• Shred cooked chicken breast and toss with grated carrots, thinly sliced cucumber and a homemade Thai peanut dressing (blend peanut butter, a splash of gluten‑free soy sauce/tamari, lime juice and a touch of honey). Serve scooped in crisp lettuce leaves.

Dinner: Mexican Beef Stuffed Bell Peppers  
• Halve large bell peppers and fill with a mixture of lean ground beef, black beans, sweet corn, diced tomatoes and mild cumin and paprika. Bake until peppers are tender. Serve with a side of plain steamed rice.

Snack: Carrot & Celery Sticks with Homemade Hummus*  
• (Make hummus using chickpeas, garlic, lemon juice and tahini but omit any onion.) Dip raw veggie sticks as desired.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 3
──────────────────────────────
Breakfast: Berry & Banana Smoothie  
• Blend lactose‑free yoghurt (or lactose‑free kefir), banana and a handful of berries with a splash of water or lactose‑free milk. Adjust sweetness with a drizzle of honey if needed.

Lunch: Grilled Steak Salad with Boiled Baby Potatoes  
• Grill a lean steak (season lightly with salt, pepper, and a sprinkle of dried oregano). Toss mixed greens with halved boiled baby potatoes and tomato wedges. Serve sliced steak on top.

Dinner: Thai Basil Chicken with Rice  
• Stir‑fry ground chicken with chopped red bell pepper and a generous handful of basil leaves. Season with a mild blend of gluten‑free soy sauce/tamari, a touch of fish sauce (if tolerated; otherwise add a squeeze of lime for umami) and a hint of chili if desired. Serve with steamed jasmine or basmati rice.

Snack: Rice Cakes with Mashed Avocado  
• Top plain rice cakes with smashed avocado and a squeeze of lime.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 4
──────────────────────────────
Breakfast: Spinach & Tomato Omelette  
• Whisk eggs with chopped spinach and diced tomato. Cook over a nonstick pan with minimal oil. Season lightly with salt and pepper.

Lunch: Mexican Chicken Quinoa Bowl  
• Toss shredded, grilled chicken with cooked quinoa, roasted corn kernels, diced tomato and chopped red bell pepper. Dress with lime juice and a sprinkle of cumin.

Dinner: Indian Lamb Curry with Potatoes  
• Gently simmer lean lamb pieces with cubed potatoes, diced tomatoes and a mild blend of turmeric, cumin and coriander. (No onions added.) Serve over basmati rice.

Snack: Cucumber

Here is a cheap, simple, and customizable 7-day meal plan tailored for an Australian adult. It focuses on budget-friendly staples (mince, chicken, potatoes, rice) and uses leftovers for lunches to save time and money. 

Because commercial spice blends and curry pastes almost always contain onion powder or gluten, this plan uses a few basic, mild pantry spices to safely create Mexican, Indian, and Thai flavor profiles.

### **The 7-Day Meal Plan**

**Day 1**
*   **Breakfast:** Certified Gluten-Free (GF) oats (or rice flakes) made with lactose-free (LF) milk, topped with sliced banana and a pinch of cinnamon.
*   **Lunch:** Quick Beef & Potato Hash (pan-fry diced potatoes and a little beef mince).
*   **Dinner:** **Mild Mexican Beef Bowls.** Beef mince cooked with diced tomatoes, mild cumin, and paprika. Served over rice with a sprinkle of LF cheese. *(Cook extra for leftovers).*
*   **Snacks:** Apple slices with peanut butter.

**Day 2**
*   **Breakfast:** 2 scrambled eggs and spinach on GF toast.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Mild Mexican Beef Bowl.
*   **Dinner:** **Mild Indian Chicken & Potatoes.** Diced chicken breast and potatoes simmered in coconut milk, turmeric, ground coriander, and fresh ginger. Served with rice. *(Cook extra for leftovers).*
*   **Snacks:** Rice crackers with a slice of LF cheese.

**Day 3**
*   **Breakfast:** Certified GF oats with LF milk and banana.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Indian Chicken & Potatoes.
*   **Dinner:** **Mexican Potato Tacos.** GF corn tortillas filled with leftover Mexican beef mince, roasted diced potatoes, and fresh tomato.
*   **Snacks:** Carrot sticks with peanut butter.

**Day 4**
*   **Breakfast:** 2 fried eggs with pan-fried tomato and GF toast.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Mexican Potato Tacos (deconstructed into a bowl).
*   **Dinner:** **Thai-style Coconut Chicken.** Chicken pan-fried with zucchini, capsicum, mild ginger, a splash of low-sodium Tamari

# 7-Day Meal Plan
### Allergy/Intolerance Safe: No shellfish · No gluten · No lactose · No onion
### Flavour profile: Mild, adjustable seasoning · Not very salty or sweet · Meat, potatoes, Mexican, Thai, Indian

**Key swaps used throughout:**
- **Onion replacement:** garlic, leek (green part only for the most sensitive), spring onion green tops, and asafoetida (gluten-free brand) for an oniony depth.
- **Lactose:** lactose-free milk, hard aged cheeses (cheddar, parmesan — naturally near-zero lactose), or plant milks.
- **Gluten:** rice, corn tortillas, GF tamari, rice noodles, potatoes.
- Season at the table so the super-taster can adjust.

---

## DAY 1
- **Breakfast:** Scrambled eggs with diced potato (pan-fried), mild paprika, fresh coriander.
- **Lunch:** Rice bowl with grilled chicken, cucumber, carrot, lime, a little GF tamari.
- **Dinner:** Mild beef tacos (corn tortillas) — seasoned beef mince with cumin & garlic, lettuce, tomato, aged cheddar.
- **Snacks:** Banana; rice crackers.

## DAY 2
- **Breakfast:** Lactose-free yoghurt with sliced pear and a few crushed nuts.
- **Lunch:** Leftover taco beef over rice with avocado.
- **Dinner:** Mild Thai chicken & rice noodle stir-fry (carrot, capsicum, green spring onion tops, garlic, lime, splash GF tamari).
- **Snacks:** Carrot sticks; boiled egg.

## DAY 3
- **Breakfast:** Potato & egg hash with mild paprika.
- **Lunch:** Chicken & cucumber rice paper rolls with lime dipping sauce (GF tamari + lime + tiny honey).
- **Dinner:** Mild Indian chicken curry (coconut milk, garlic, cumin, turmeric, mild curry powder) with rice.
- **Snacks:** Apple slices; rice crackers.

## DAY 4
- **Breakfast:** Lactose-free yoghurt with banana.
- **Lunch:** Leftover chicken curry with rice.
- **Dinner:** Baked potatoes topped with seasoned beef mince, aged cheddar, fresh tomato.
- **Snacks:** Cucumber slices; handful of nuts.

## DAY 5
- **Breakfast:** Eggs any style with grilled tomato and rice cakes.
- **Lunch:** Beef & rice bowl (leftover mince) with lettuce, avocado, lime.
- **Dinner:** Thai-style mild pork & vegetable rice (carrot, capsicum, garlic, coriander, lime).
- **Snacks:** Pear; rice crackers.

## DAY 6
- **Breakfast:** Potato hash with eggs and coriander.
- **Lunch:** Leftover Thai pork rice.
- **Dinner:** Mexican-style chicken & rice skillet (tomato, capsicum, cumin, garlic, mild paprika), aged cheddar on top.
- **Snacks:** Banana; boiled egg.

## DAY 7
- **Breakfast:** Lactose-free yoghurt with pear and nuts.
- **Lunch:** Leftover Mexican chicken rice with avocado.
- **Dinner:** Indian-style mild dahl (red lentils, coconut milk, garlic, cumin, turmeric) with rice and roasted potato.
- **Snacks:** Apple; rice crackers.

---

## GROCERY LIST

**Proteins**
- Eggs (1 dozen)
- Chicken thighs/breast (~1.5 kg)
- Beef mince (~800 g)
- Pork mince or strips (~400 g)
- Red lentils (1 bag)

**Carbs / Potatoes**
- Rice (white or basmati, large bag)
- Potatoes (~2 kg)
- Corn tortillas (GF, check label)
- Rice paper sheets
- Rice noodles
- Rice crackers / rice cakes

**Vegetables & Fruit**
- Carrots, cucumbers, capsicums, lettuce, tomatoes
- Avocados (2–3)
- Spring onions (green tops only)
- Garlic, fresh ginger
- Coriander, limes
- Bananas, apples, pears

**Dairy / Alternatives**
- Lactose-free yoghurt
- Aged cheddar
- Lactose-free or coconut milk

**Pantry / Seasoning**
- Coconut milk (cans)
- GF tamari (soy sauce alternative)
- Olive or neutral oil
- Mild curry powder, ground cumin, turmeric, mild paprika
- Asafoetida (GF) — optional onion-flavour booster
- Salt & pepper (added at table)
- Unsalted nuts
- Small jar honey

---

## PREP NOTES
- **Batch cook:** Roast a tray of potatoes and cook a big pot of rice early in the week; both reheat well and anchor several meals.
- **Cook proteins ahead:** Brown beef mince (Day 1) doubles for Days 2, 4, 5. Curry (Day 3) covers Day 4.
- **Seasoning strategy:** Keep spice quantities low when cooking; provide salt, lime, chilli, and extra spice at the table so it's adjustable for super-taster sensitivity.
- **Onion-free flavour base:** Sauté garlic gently (don't brown) plus a pinch of asafoetida and green spring onion tops to build savoury depth.
- **Label checks:** Confirm tortillas, tamari, curry powder, and asafoetida are certified gluten-free; check spice blends don't contain onion powder.
- **Cost savers:** Buy chicken thighs, mince, rice, lentils, and potatoes in bulk; use seasonal vegetables; rely on dried spices over jarred sauces.

*Note: This is general guidance, not medical advice. With diagnosed allergies, always read every label and consult your doctor or dietitian, especially regarding cross-contamination.*

# 7-Day Meal Plan
### For a 31-Year-Old Australian Adult
**Allergies/Intolerances:** Shellfish, Gluten, Lactose, Onion
**Preferences:** Meat, Potatoes, Mexican, Thai, Indian flavours
**Sensitivity:** Super-taster — mild, adjustable seasoning throughout
**All prices approximate AUD, budget-focused**

---

> **A few quick notes before you start:**
> - "Adjustable seasoning" means spices are added lightly during cooking — keep extra on the table so you can add more to taste
> - Coconut milk used throughout is full-fat canned (naturally lactose-free)
> - All soy sauce must be **tamari** (gluten-free soy sauce) — check the label
> - Check all packaged goods (stock, spice blends, sauces) for hidden gluten, onion powder, and lactose
> - Leek greens and the green tops of spring onions/scallions can substitute for onion flavour if tolerated — omit if not
> - Garlic is included as a flavour base; omit if you are also sensitive to other alliums
> - Batch cooking on Day 1 and Day 4 saves significant time

---

## DAY 1 — Sunday (Batch Cook Day)

### Breakfast
**Scrambled Eggs with Roasted Potato Hash**
- 3 eggs scrambled in olive oil
- 1 medium potato, diced small and pan-fried until golden
- Fresh tomato sliced on the side
- Season lightly with salt, pepper, and a pinch of smoked paprika

### Lunch
**Simple Chicken and Rice Bowl**
- 1 cup cooked jasmine rice
- Grilled or pan-fried chicken thigh (seasoned with garlic, cumin, mild paprika)
- Sliced cucumber and carrot sticks
- Drizzle of olive oil and a squeeze of lemon

### Dinner
**Mild Indian-Style Chicken Curry** *(make a double batch — saves Day 2 lunch)*
- Chicken thighs simmered in canned tomatoes, coconut milk, garlic, ginger, mild curry powder, cumin, coriander
- Served over jasmine rice
- Garnish with fresh coriander if desired

### Snacks
- A small handful of plain rice crackers
- A banana
- A small handful of unsalted mixed nuts

---

## DAY 2 — Monday

### Breakfast
**Banana Oat Smoothie** *(use certified gluten-free oats)*
- 1 banana
- ½ cup gluten-free rolled oats
- 1 cup coconut milk (from carton, not full-fat canned)
- Blend until smooth
- Optional: a tiny pinch of cinnamon

### Lunch
**Leftover Chicken Curry with Rice**
- Reheated from Day 1 dinner
- Add extra fresh coriander or a squeeze of lime to freshen it up

### Dinner
**Mexican-Style Beef Mince Taco Bowl** *(use corn tortillas or rice — no wheat tortillas)*
- Beef mince cooked with garlic, cumin, mild chilli powder, canned diced tomatoes
- Served over rice or with corn tortillas
- Toppings: shredded lettuce, diced tomato, sliced avocado, squeeze of lime
- Optional: a small dollop of dairy-free sour cream or plain coconut yoghurt

### Snacks
- Apple slices
- Rice crackers with avocado mashed on top
- Small handful of unsalted cashews

---

## DAY 3 — Tuesday

### Breakfast
**Fried Eggs on Gluten-Free Toast**
- 2 eggs fried in olive oil
- 2 slices gluten-free bread, toasted
- Sliced tomato on the side
- Light seasoning with salt and pepper

### Lunch
**Thai-Style Chicken and Rice Noodle Salad**
- Rice noodles (cooked and cooled)
- Shredded cooked chicken (use rotisserie or leftover grilled chicken)
- Shredded carrot, cucumber, fresh coriander
- Dressing: 1 tbsp tamari, juice of 1 lime, 1 tsp sesame oil, tiny pinch of sugar, small amount of fresh ginger — mix and drizzle lightly
- Optional: a few crushed unsalted peanuts on top

### Dinner
**Baked Lemon Herb Chicken Thighs with Roasted Potatoes and Vegetables**
- Chicken thighs marinated in lemon juice, garlic, olive oil, dried oregano, mild paprika
- Roasted alongside diced potatoes, zucchini, and capsicum
- Roast at 200°C for 35–40 minutes
- *(Make extra chicken for Day 4 lunch)*

### Snacks
- Carrot and cucumber sticks
- A small handful of plain rice crackers
- A piece of fruit (orange or pear)

---

## DAY 4 — Wednesday (Second Batch Cook Day)

### Breakfast
**Gluten-Free Oat Porridge**
- ½ cup certified gluten-free rolled oats cooked with water or coconut milk (carton)
- Top with sliced banana and a drizzle of maple syrup (very light)
- Optional: pinch of cinnamon

### Lunch
**Leftover Chicken and Potato Wrap** *(use corn tortillas)*
- Shredded leftover chicken from Day 3
- Sliced roasted potato
- Lettuce, tomato, avocado
- Light squeeze of lemon or lime

### Dinner
**Thai Green Curry with Chicken and Vegetables** *(make a double batch — saves Day 5 lunch)*
- Use a mild Thai green curry paste *(check label: must be gluten-free and onion-free — Mae Ploy or Maesri brands are generally suitable, but always verify)*
- Chicken thighs, zucchini, capsicum, baby spinach
- Simmered in canned coconut milk with a splash of tamari and lime juice
- Served over jasmine rice
- Fresh coriander and lime wedge to serve

### Snacks
- Rice crackers with mashed avocado
- A banana
- Small handful of unsalted mixed nuts

---

## DAY 5 — Thursday

### Breakfast
**Scrambled Eggs with Smoked Paprika and Roasted Tomatoes**
- 3 eggs scrambled in olive oil
- 2 tomatoes halved and roasted in the oven at 180°C for 15 minutes
- Season with a small pinch of smoked paprika, salt, and pepper
- Serve with gluten-free toast if desired

### Lunch
**Leftover Thai Green Curry with Rice**
- Reheated from Day 4 dinner
- Add a fresh squeeze of lime and extra coriander

### Dinner
**Indian-Style Lamb and Potato Curry** *(mild)*
- Diced lamb shoulder or lamb mince cooked with garlic, ginger, canned tomatoes, mild curry powder, cumin, turmeric, coriander powder
- Add diced potato and simmer until tender (about 25–30 minutes)
- Served over jasmine rice
- Garnish with fresh coriander
- *(Make extra for Day 6 lunch)*

### Snacks
- Apple or pear
- Plain rice crackers
- Small handful of unsalted peanuts or cashews

---

## DAY 6 — Friday

### Breakfast
**Coconut Chia Pudding** *(prep the night before)*
- 3 tbsp chia seeds soaked overnight in 1 cup canned coconut milk
- Top with sliced banana or mango in the morning
- Optional: tiny drizzle of maple syrup

### Lunch
**Leftover Lamb and Potato Curry with Rice**
- Reheated from Day 5 dinner
- Serve with fresh coriander and a squeeze of lemon

### Dinner
**Mexican-Style Beef and Bean Bowl**
- Beef mince cooked with garlic, cumin, mild chilli powder, canned black beans (drained and rinsed), canned diced tomatoes
- Served over rice
- Toppings: diced avocado, shredded lettuce, fresh tomato, lime juice, fresh coriander
- Optional: corn tortillas on the side

### Snacks
- Carrot sticks with a small amount of hummus *(check label for gluten and onion — or make your own: blended chickpeas, tahini, lemon, garlic, olive oil)*
- A small handful of unsalted nuts
- A piece of fruit

---

## DAY 7 — Saturday (Relaxed Day)

### Breakfast
**Potato and Egg Frittata** *(great for using up leftover vegetables)*
- 4 eggs whisked with a splash of coconut milk (carton), salt, pepper
- Add diced cooked potato, capsicum, spinach, and any leftover vegetables
- Cook in an oven-safe pan on the stovetop until edges set, then finish under the grill for 5 minutes
- Serve with sliced tomato

### Lunch
**Simple Thai-Style Rice Noodle Soup**
- Rice noodles in a light broth made from chicken stock (gluten-free), ginger, garlic, a splash of tamari, lime juice
- Add shredded cooked chicken, bok choy or spinach, sliced carrot
- Top with fresh coriander and a lime wedge
- Optional: a very small pinch of chilli flakes on the side for those who want heat

### Dinner
**Baked Salmon with Roasted Sweet Potato and Greens**
- Salmon fillet baked at 200°C for 15–18 minutes
- Marinate briefly in tamari, sesame oil, ginger, and a squeeze of lime
- Roasted sweet potato wedges alongside
- Steamed broccolini or green beans
- Serve with jasmine rice if desired

### Snacks
- Rice crackers with avocado
- A banana or mango
- Small handful of unsal
